key_rule: Determine if patient is alert and not vomiting blood
Identify breathing difficulty or facial swelling early
Determine animal type and severity of bleeding
Confirm scene is safe before EMS arrival
Rule out aortic / cardiac referral patterns
Differentiate ineffective breathing from agonal
Estimate total body surface area and inhalation injury risk
Get all occupants out of structure before further questions
Begin telephone CPR if breathing absent or agonal
Treat as STEMI risk until proven otherwise
Distinguish complete vs partial airway obstruction
Position on side after seizure if not breathing well
Confirm caller can swallow before any oral glucose
Begin rescue breathing as first priority
Ensure power source disconnected before approach
Do not allow rubbing irrigate with water if chemical
Screen for cervical spine concern in elderly or height
Identify thunderclap or worst-ever pattern as time critical
Distinguish cardiac symptoms from device discharge events
Move to temperature-neutral environment before assessment
Direct pressure with clean cloth tourniquet for limb only
Confirm patient airway position if conscious
Identify substance dose and time of ingestion
Distinguish imminent delivery from labor complications
Confirm scene safety and weapon status before approach
Stratify by alteration of consciousness and breathing
Apply direct pressure not removal of impaling object
Use FAST exam Face Arm Speech Time
Capture sending facility and receiving destination
Identify mechanism and number of casualties
Confirm breathing and pulse status before further questions
